system_overview: The system works as middleware between the vehicle speed signal and the audio management system. It receives vehicle speed data and adjusts the media volume accordingly using predefined rules.
scope:
The system monitors vehicle speed and dynamically changes the media volume level.
Included Features-
Real-time vehicle speed monitoring
Automatic media volume adjustment
Smooth volume transition
Manual volume override
Basic UI for speed and volume display
Logging and debugging support
purpose: The system aims to improve driver comfort and safety by reducing the need for manual volume adjustments during driving. The project is to develop a system that automatically adjusts the media volume in a vehicle based on the vehicle’s speed.
intended_audience: Product stakeholders, software developers, integration engineers, test engineers, reviewers, and quality teams.
diagnostics: Diagnostics include The system shall log speed changes, volume adjustments, manual overrides, and system errors.
acceptance_criteria: The SRS is accepted when all identified requirements are implemented, verified, and validated against the documented functional, performance, reliability, usability, safety, and constraint expectations.
operating_environment:
Vehicle infotainment system
Android/Linux-based automotive platform
Audio Manager APIs
Vehicle HAL / CAN Interface
assumptions: System has permission to control media volume.

requirements.purpose: The system shall continuously receive vehicle speed data from the Vehicle HAL/CAN interface.
requirements.process: Receive vehicle speed data from the vehicle interface and update the internal speed value continuously.
requirements.validation: Verify by review, unit testing, integration testing, or system testing that the system shall continuously receive vehicle speed data from the Vehicle HAL/CAN interface.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Accepted when The system shall continuously receive vehicle speed data from the Vehicle HAL/CAN interface is demonstrated under the specified operating conditions and the expected output is observed: Updated speed value.
requirements.mandatory_fields: Mandatory fields: inputs (Vehicle speed signal); expected outputs (Updated speed value); triggering request/message/signal.
requirements.pre_loaded_values: No source-specific pre-loaded values are identified; use approved project configuration and interface initialization values where applicable.
requirements.default_values: Use project-approved default values for optional configuration; mandatory runtime values must be supplied or validated before processing.
requirements.valid_range_of_values: Valid values are the calibrated sensor/interface range and threshold limits defined for the target operating environment.
requirements.data_latency_period: Fulfillment time is within the allocated control-loop or signal-processing cycle for this requirement.
requirements.data_retention_period: Retain runtime data only for the active transaction unless project diagnostics, logging, or configuration storage requires longer retention.
requirements.data_rate: Data rate follows each relevant signal update or state-machine execution cycle.
requirements.external_events: A sensor update, network/interface message, or connected subsystem state change triggers this functionality.
requirements.temporal_events: Periodic scheduling and timeout monitoring apply when required by the software architecture or interface timing.
requirements.constraints: Implementation constraints: conform to the referenced interface/API specification.
requirements.effects_on_other_systems: Affects downstream components or users that consume the requirement output: Updated speed value.
requirements.assumptions: Input signals, referenced interfaces, configuration data, and dependent services are available under the documented operating conditions.
requirements.failure_scenario: Failure occurs if required inputs are invalid, unavailable, delayed, or out of range, preventing the system from confirming that the system shall continuously receive vehicle speed data from the Vehicle HAL/CAN interface.
requirements.action_on_failure: On failure, reject invalid input where applicable, preserve or move to a safe/default state, report the fault, and make diagnostic evidence available for verification.
requirements.testability: Yes. Test during unit, integration, and system validation by executing requirement-level test cases and confirming that the system shall continuously receive vehicle speed data from the Vehicle HAL/CAN interface.
requirements.comments: Completed from source context and requirement-level engineering inference.

requirements.purpose: The system shall automatically adjust media volume based on vehicle speed.
requirements.inputs: Vehicle speed signal from Vehicle HAL/CAN interface and current media volume level.
requirements.outputs: Adjusted media volume level based on vehicle speed.
requirements.process: Read vehicle speed, calculate the target media volume using predefined rules, and apply the volume change.
requirements.validation: Verify by review, unit testing, integration testing, or system testing that the system shall automatically adjust media volume based on vehicle speed.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Accepted when The system shall automatically adjust media volume based on vehicle speed is demonstrated under the specified operating conditions.

requirements.purpose: The system shall gradually adjust volume to avoid sudden changes.

requirements.outputs: Smoothly transitioned media volume without abrupt changes.

The system shall gradually adjust volume to avoid sudden changes.
comments.fallback_reason: Inferred from requirement text and document context

requirements.inputs: Driver manual volume adjustment event and current automatic adjustment state.
requirements.outputs: Automatic adjustment paused temporarily and driver-selected volume preserved.
requirements.process: Detect manual volume changes, pause automatic adjustment temporarily, and preserve the user's selected volume.
requirements.validation: Verify by review, unit testing, integration testing, or system testing that automatic adjustment shall pause temporarily.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Accepted when Automatic adjustment shall pause temporarily is demonstrated under the specified operating conditions.
requirements.mandatory_fields: Mandatory fields: inputs (Driver manual volume adjustment event and current automatic adjustment state.); expected outputs (Automatic adjustment paused temporarily and driver-selected volume preserved.).
requirements.pre_loaded_values: No source-specific pre-loaded values are identified; use approved project configuration and interface initialization values where applicable.
requirements.default_values: Use project-approved default values for optional configuration; mandatory runtime values must be supplied or validated before processing.
requirements.valid_range_of_values: Valid values are constrained by the applicable interface specification, configuration data, and operating-environment limits.
requirements.data_latency_period: Fulfillment time is within the project-defined transaction or task scheduling budget for this function.
requirements.data_retention_period: Retain runtime data only for the active transaction unless project diagnostics, logging, or configuration storage requires longer retention.
requirements.data_rate: Transaction rate is event-driven and occurs for each valid external request or command.
requirements.external_events: Relevant external user actions, interface messages, commands, or subsystem state changes that require automatic adjustment shall pause temporarily.
requirements.temporal_events: Periodic scheduling and timeout monitoring apply when required by the software architecture or interface timing.
requirements.constraints: Implementation must satisfy the documented operating environment, interface contracts, timing budgets, safety/security rules, and project delivery constraints.
requirements.effects_on_other_systems: Affects downstream components or users that consume the requirement output: Automatic adjustment paused temporarily and driver-selected volume preserved..
requirements.assumptions: Input signals, referenced interfaces, configuration data, and dependent services are available under the documented operating conditions.
requirements.failure_scenario: Failure occurs if required inputs are invalid, unavailable, delayed, or out of range, preventing the system from confirming that automatic adjustment shall pause temporarily.
requirements.action_on_failure: On failure, reject invalid input where applicable, preserve or move to a safe/default state, report the fault, and make diagnostic evidence available for verification.
requirements.testability: Yes. Test during unit, integration, and system validation by executing requirement-level test cases and confirming that automatic adjustment shall pause temporarily.
requirements.comments: Completed from source context and requirement-level engineering inference.

requirements.purpose: The system shall log speed changes, volume adjustments, manual overrides, and system errors.
requirements.inputs: Speed changes, volume adjustments, manual override events, and system error events.
requirements.outputs: Log entries for speed changes, volume adjustments, manual overrides, and system errors.
requirements.process: Capture speed, volume, manual override, and error events, then write them to the system log.
requirements.validation: Verify by review, unit testing, integration testing, or system testing that the system shall log speed changes, volume adjustments, manual overrides, and system errors.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Accepted when The system shall log speed changes, volume adjustments, manual overrides, and system errors is demonstrated under the specified operating conditions.
requirements.mandatory_fields: Mandatory fields: inputs (Speed changes, volume adjustments, manual override events, and system error events.); expected outputs (Log entries for speed changes, volume adjustments, manual overrides, and system errors.).
requirements.pre_loaded_values: No source-specific pre-loaded values are identified; use approved project configuration and interface initialization values where applicable.
requirements.default_values: Use project-approved default values for optional configuration; mandatory runtime values must be supplied or validated before processing.
requirements.valid_range_of_values: Valid values are the calibrated sensor/interface range and threshold limits defined for the target operating environment.
requirements.data_latency_period: Fulfillment time is within the project-defined transaction or task scheduling budget for this function.
requirements.data_retention_period: Retain log/audit records according to the project diagnostic and data-retention policy.
requirements.data_rate: Transaction rate is event-driven and occurs for each valid external request or command.
requirements.external_events: Relevant external user actions, interface messages, commands, or subsystem state changes that require the system shall log speed changes, volume adjustments, manual overrides, and system errors.
requirements.temporal_events: Periodic scheduling and timeout monitoring apply when required by the software architecture or interface timing.
requirements.constraints: Implementation must satisfy the documented operating environment, interface contracts, timing budgets, safety/security rules, and project delivery constraints.
requirements.effects_on_other_systems: Affects downstream components or users that consume the requirement output: Log entries for speed changes, volume adjustments, manual overrides, and system errors..
requirements.assumptions: Input signals, referenced interfaces, configuration data, and dependent services are available under the documented operating conditions.
requirements.failure_scenario: Failure occurs if required inputs are invalid, unavailable, delayed, or out of range, preventing the system from confirming that the system shall log speed changes, volume adjustments, manual overrides, and system errors.
requirements.action_on_failure: On failure, reject invalid input where applicable, preserve or move to a safe/default state, report the fault, and make diagnostic evidence available for verification.
requirements.testability: Yes. Test during unit, integration, and system validation by executing requirement-level test cases and confirming that the system shall log speed changes, volume adjustments, manual overrides, and system errors.
requirements.comments: Completed from source context and requirement-level engineering inference.
